      SCRIPT OPTIONS GO HERE


      A. "[  FIRST NAME  ],


      I came across your background as an [  ROLE  ] at [  COMPANY  ]  & was impressed by your tenure in sales. I’m currently building my career in tech sales & would really value connecting with you to learn from your experience & perspective!


      Looking forward to connecting!"


      B. "[  FIRST NAME  ], I saw you’re an [  ROLE  ] at [  COMPANY  ] & given your track record, I’d love to connect! I’m actively developing in tech sales and am especially interested in understanding what’s driven consistent success in roles like yours.


      Would appreciate being part of your network!"


      C. I'm always interested in meeting people who’ve built strong longevity in sales roles. I’m breaking into tech sales and would enjoy connecting and learning what’s made the biggest difference in your journey as a [  ROLE  ]?


      How do you like working at [  COMPANY  ]?"



      Initial Contact On LinkedIn
      Second Attempt w No Connection on LinkedIn

      SCRIPT OPTIONS GO HERE


      A. "Hi [  FIRST NAME  ],


      Wanted to follow up in case my earlier note got buried. I’m currently breaking into tech sales and was really hoping to connect to learn from your experience as an [  ROLE  ] at [  COMPANY  ].


      If you’re open to it, I’d value a quick 15-minute conversation to better understand what success looks like on your in your sales org.


      Appreciate you considering!"


      B. "Hi [  FIRST NAME  ],


      I know you likely get a lot of messages, so I’ll keep this quick, I'm building my foundation in tech sales and looking to learn directly from experienced sales professionals like yourself.


      I’d love to connect and, if open, grab 15 minutes to hear what’s made you successful at [  COMPANY  ]."


      C (Recruiter Only). "[  FIRST NAME  ],


      I wanted to follow up in case my earlier note got missed. I’ve been exploring GTM roles and your work building sales teams stood out to me.


      I’d really value 15 minutes to better understand what you look for in top candidates and how success is defined for [  ROLE  ]s at [  COMPANY  ].


      Are you open to a coffee chat?"


      D (Manager Only). "Hi [  FIRST NAME  ],


      Obviously reaching out once more! I’m currently transitioning in tech sales & have been researching your team.


      I would really value 15 minutes to understand how you evaluate [  ROLE  ]s, what ramp looks like, and what separates top performers on your team.


      Happy to work around your schedule!


      Rheonda O."


      E (Recruiter Only). "Good Day [  FIRST NAME  ],


      My name is Rheonda Ogletree, and I’m a tenacious problem-solver actively pursuing a career in competitive tech sales.


      With a strong interest in your organization, I recently applied for the [  ROLE  ] position (Req ID: [ ####### ] ) and wanted to follow up directly. Given your experience building high-performing sales teams, I would greatly value the opportunity to connect and better understand what you look for in top candidates and how success is defined in this role.


      If you’re open to it, I’d appreciate 15 minutes of your time for a brief conversation. I’m happy to work around your schedule.


      Looking forward to hearing from you,
      Rheonda Ogletree"


      F (Manager Only). "Good Day [  FIRST NAME  ],


      My name is Rheonda Ogletree, and I’m currently pursuing opportunities in tech sales with a strong focus on long-term performance and growth.


      I came across your leadership experience and wanted to reach out, as I’d value your perspective on how your team operates. Specifically, I’m interested in understanding what you expect from new hires, how ramp is approached, and what separates top performers over time.


      If you’re open to it, I’d greatly appreciate 15 minutes to learn from your experience and better align how I can make an immediate impact in the tech sales industry.


      I look forward to hearing from you,
      Rheonda Ogletree

      Second Attempt w No Connection on LinkedIn
      Follow-Up #1 on LinkedIn

      SCRIPT OPTIONS GO HERE


      A. "[  FIRST NAME  ],


      Appreciate you connecting! I wanted to follow up, I'd love to get your perspective on what success looks like as an  [  ROLE  ] at [  COMPANY  ] and what separates top performers.


      Would you be open to a quick 15-minute chat this week or next? I’d come prepared with a few focused questions to gain your industry insights."


      B. "Thank you so much for connecting!


      I’ve been looking into a [  ROLE  ]  position at [  COMPANY  ] & I’d really value your firsthand perspective on the team & what’s driving success there.


      I’d appreciate 15 minutes to learn from you & I'm totally flexible on time.


      Either way, glad to be connected!"


      C. "I appreciate you connecting! As I'm moving further into tech sales, I’m intentionally using this month to improve my understanding of how [  ROLE  ]s ramp up quickly, make an early impact & create realized value at companies like [  COMPANY  ] .


      I’d love to grab 15 minutes to learn from your experience, especially around what’s made you successful there!


      Also glad to share a bit about myself & my background if helpful."

      Follow-Up #1 on LinkedIn
      Follow-Up #2 on LinkedIn
      Follow-Up #2 On LinkedIn

      SCRIPT OPTIONS GO HERE (3 Max)


      A. Would you be open to a quick 15 From your experience, what are the core expectations leadership has for [  ROLE  ]s & [  ROLE  ]s beyond just hitting quota? What does day-to-day actually look like? And how would you describe the quality of your workload?


      B. How would you describe the work-life balance on the team, especially during heavier sales cycles, and how does the culture support (or challenge) that?


      C. What tends to keep the team motivated? Are there specific incentives, leadership styles, or team dynamics that really drive performance?


      D. How attainable have targets been over the past year, and what separates the reps who consistently hit or exceed them from those who don’t?


      E. What has the past year been like for the team in terms of growth, wins, or changes & based on that, do you feel someone coming in as an SDR right now would be well-positioned to succeed & grow into an [  ROLE  ] role?
